title: Value Proposition
how_to_read_this_file: Value at AquaVision is not one proposition, it is three, layered by phase and audience. Phase 1 sells to beginners on free education, guidance, and organization. Phase 2 sells to growing farms on ROI-positive analytics, marketplace access, and expert time. Phase 3 sells to commercial and enterprise operators on the hardware value prop this repository originally led with: earlier detection, avoided mass mortality, and aeration savings. Each phase keeps its own value hierarchy ranked by how easily a sceptical buyer can verify the claim — the discipline that made the original hardware pitch credible is applied here to the software claims too, not relaxed for them.

phase_1_foundation_value.audience: Beginners and smallholders establishing their first ponds, most of whom have never used any farm-management tool before, digital or paper.
phase_1_foundation_value.headline_value: Free education, guidance, and organization for people who currently have none of the three.

value_proposition_canvas.note: Mapped against a beginner persona, not 'the customer' in general. No beginner farmer has been interviewed for this canvas; it is drawn from the problem taxonomy and should be treated as a hypothesis, not a finding.

customer_jobs.job: Learn how to set up and run a pond without an apprenticeship
Know what to feed, when, and how much
Recognise a sick or dying fish before the whole pond is lost
Ask someone who has done this before
Keep some record of what happened, for their own sake and for any buyer who asks

pains.pain: No formal training and no affordable expert to ask
Water quality is invisible without equipment they cannot afford
Feeding and stocking decisions are guesswork copied from a neighbour
A disease outbreak is often identified too late to act on
Nothing is written down, so nothing this cycle informs the next one

gains.gain: A guide that answers a question at 9 p.m. when no extension officer is reachable
A feeding schedule instead of a guess
An early photo-based opinion on a sick fish
A community of people who have the same problems
A record they can actually find again next season

pain_relievers.reliever: AI farming guide answers common setup and husbandry questions on demand
pain_relievers.addresses: No formal training and no affordable expert

pain_relievers.reliever: Manual/photo water quality logging turns invisible conditions into a written trend, even without sensors
pain_relievers.addresses: Water quality is invisible without equipment

pain_relievers.reliever: Photo-based disease ID gives a first opinion faster than travelling to find one
Record keeping accumulates automatically as a by-product of using the app

gain_creators.creator: Learning modules structure knowledge a beginner would otherwise assemble from scattered, inconsistent sources
Community discussion supplies the social proof and troubleshooting a solo smallholder has no other access to
Being free removes the single largest adoption barrier this segment has for any of it

fit_assessment.reasoning: The relievers map onto plausible pains for a segment nobody currently serves well. But the app has not launched, no beginner farmer has used it, and 'AI farming guide' and 'photo-based disease ID' are design targets, not measured capabilities. A canvas this early is a hypothesis about a persona, not evidence about a customer.

the_value_hierarchy.note: Ranked by how easily a beginner farmer with no budget and no technical background can verify the claim themselves, cheapest and most checkable first.

tiers.claim: Structured record keeping, in place of a notebook or nothing at all
tiers.verifiability: HIGH — the farmer can see their own logs accumulate from day one; there is nothing to take on faith

tiers.claim: A feeding schedule and a farm setup checklist instead of copying a neighbour
tiers.verifiability: HIGH — the schedule is visible and the farmer can compare it against what they were already doing

tiers.claim: A faster first opinion on a sick or distressed fish via photo-based disease ID
tiers.verifiability: MEDIUM — the farmer can check the AI's opinion against what a vet or experienced neighbour later says, when one is reachable
tiers.honest_note: No model has been trained or validated yet. Until there is a held-out test set and real farmer photos, this claim is a design target, not an accuracy figure.

tiers.claim: The AI farming guide answers a real husbandry question correctly
tiers.verifiability: LOW-MEDIUM — correctness depends on the question and is hard for a beginner to check against anything but outcomes weeks later

the_value_hierarchy.the_strategic_conclusion: Lead with the boring, checkable tier 1 and 2 claims — organization and structure — exactly as the hardware pitch led with the electricity bill. Do not oversell the AI guidance tiers to a beginner who has no way to verify them until a season has passed.

phase_1_foundation_value.honest_note: Phase 1 has no users yet. Every claim above is a design target or an assumption; none is measured. `traction.json` stays at honest zero until real farmers are using the app.

phase_2_monetization_value.audience: Farms that have grown past what free, manual tracking supports and are willing to pay for an edge — the conversion point from Phase 1.
phase_2_monetization_value.headline_value: ROI-positive analytics, marketplace access, and expert time that a growing farm can check against its own numbers.

value_proposition_canvas.note: Mapped against a growing small-commercial farm owner deciding whether to upgrade from the free app. No such farmer has been interviewed; this is drawn from the problem taxonomy, same caveat as Phase 1.

customer_jobs.job: Turn a season of logged data into a decision, not just an archive
Buy feed and equipment without a middleman markup
Reach a real vet or aquaculturist without travelling
Justify the subscription cost to themselves or a co-owner

pains.pain: A season of logs sits unused because nobody has time to analyse it by hand
Feed and equipment sourcing is fragmented and locally price-gouged
Expert advice means a long trip or an expensive one-off visit
No way to tell whether a recommendation actually helped

gains.gain: A dashboard that turns logged history into a trend and a recommendation
Marketplace prices they can compare against the local supplier
A consultation booked in minutes instead of a day's travel
Evidence the subscription paid for itself this cycle

pain_relievers.reliever: Premium analytics surfaces trends in the farmer's own Phase 1 data without manual analysis
Marketplace aggregates feed/equipment/buyer listings for price comparison
Paid expert consultations connect a farmer to a vet or aquaculturist remotely
pain_relievers.addresses: Expert advice means a long trip

gain_creators.creator: Predictive health monitoring flags a developing problem before it is obvious, if the underlying model holds up
Smart recommendations turn accumulated history into a suggestion tailored to this farm rather than generic advice

fit_assessment.reasoning: The relievers are coherent on paper, but this whole tier is contingent on Phase 1 first accumulating real data at scale, and on the marketplace clearing its two-sided cold start. Neither has happened. This canvas demonstrates internal consistency, not customer fit.

the_value_hierarchy.note: Ranked by how easily a paying, growing farm can verify the claim without trusting AquaVision's modelling.

tiers.claim: Marketplace prices are visibly competitive against the farmer's current supplier
tiers.verifiability: HIGH — the farmer already knows their current price and can compare line by line
tiers.honest_note: Depends on the marketplace having enough sellers listed to be competitive at all — the two-sided bootstrap problem is unresolved.

tiers.claim: An expert consultation is faster and cheaper than the farmer's current alternative
tiers.verifiability: HIGH — travel time and current consultation cost are both things the farmer already knows

tiers.claim: Premium analytics surfaces a trend the farmer had not noticed in their own logged data
tiers.verifiability: MEDIUM — checkable by the farmer looking back at their own numbers, but requires them to have logged consistently in Phase 1 first
tiers.proof_horizon: one season with consistent Phase 1 logging

tiers.claim: Predictive health monitoring and smart recommendations improve outcomes measurably
tiers.verifiability: LOW-MEDIUM — attribution is hard without a controlled comparison, the same problem the hardware FCR claim has
tiers.honest_note: No model has been trained on real farm data yet, because Phase 1 has not accumulated any. This is the most exciting Phase 2 claim and the least demonstrable today — do not lead with it.

the_value_hierarchy.the_strategic_conclusion: Sell the marketplace and consultations first — they are checkable against a price and a calendar the farmer already has. Earn the analytics claim once there is a season of real logs behind it. Do not promise predictive accuracy before a trained model exists to measure.

phase_2_monetization_value.honest_note: Phase 2 has no subscribers yet and cannot have any until Phase 1 exists and has run long enough to produce data worth analysing. The conversion-funnel assumption (free users upgrade at some rate) is itself unproven and should be named as an assumption in financial projections, not treated as a given.

phase_3_smart_aquaculture_value.audience: Commercial and enterprise operators whose scale justifies automated sensing.
phase_3_smart_aquaculture_value.headline_value: Earlier detection, avoided mass mortality, and aeration savings — the value proposition this repository originally led with, now scoped explicitly to the Phase 3 hardware layer rather than presented as the whole company.

value_proposition_canvas.note: Mapped against the Phase 3 beachhead persona (Marissa, commercial farm operations manager). A canvas mapped against 'the customer' in general is mapped against nobody.

customer_jobs.job: Hit the survival and FCR targets this cycle
Explain performance variance to the owner
Pass the certification audit without a fire drill
Not be the manager on duty when the fish die

pains.pain: Aeration runs on a schedule nobody can justify
Feed is 60 percent of cost with no feedback loop
Finds out about a problem when fish are already piping
Cannot explain why survival moved 16 points between cycles

gains.gain: A number to put in front of the owner
Lower kWh at equal or better DO
Knowing the water is fine, at 4 a.m., without going out

pain_relievers.reliever: Multi-station profiling several times daily replaces the impossible manual round
DO data lets aeration run on evidence rather than on a timer
pain_relievers.addresses: Aeration runs on a schedule nobody can justify

pain_relievers.reliever: Pre-dawn sorties measure the diel minimum, which is the number that matters and the one nobody has
pain_relievers.addresses: Finds out when fish are piping

pain_relievers.reliever: Automated logging produces the audit record as a by-product

gain_creators.creator: Depth-resolved profiles reveal stratification that no surface method can see
Probe-calibrated imagery maps conditions across the whole array, not one point
Dispensing converts a finding into an action without a boat
Accumulated site history means each cycle starts smarter than the last

fit_assessment.reasoning: The relievers map onto the pains coherently. But every reliever is a design target, not a measured capability, and the customer whose pains these are has not been interviewed. A value proposition canvas built without customer contact demonstrates internal consistency, not fit.

the_value_hierarchy.note: Ranked by how easily a sceptical buyer can verify the claim themselves. This ordering is deliberately the inverse of how exciting each claim is — and it is the correct order to sell in.

tiers.claim: Fewer aerator-hours at equal or better dissolved oxygen
tiers.verifiability: HIGH — electricity is metered monthly, already budgeted, and the farm can check it without trusting us at all
tiers.why_lead_with_this: It is boring, checkable, and requires zero belief in our modelling. A buyer who verifies this claim will extend credit to the others. A buyer who is asked to believe the mortality claim first has nothing to check and will discount everything.
tiers.status: unproven but testable immediately in a pilot

tiers.claim: Environmental records compile themselves for certification audits
tiers.verifiability: HIGH — the farm currently pays someone hours to do this by hand
tiers.status: a straightforward consequence of logging; the lowest-risk claim in the product

tiers.claim: Measurable FCR improvement through condition-aware feeding
tiers.verifiability: MEDIUM — FCR is measurable but noisy, and attribution to us requires a controlled comparison
tiers.proof_horizon: one to two cycles with a control
tiers.economic_anchor: At USD 500/t feed, a 0.1 FCR improvement is worth USD 50 per tonne produced
tiers.honest_note: The arithmetic is externally sourced and solid. Whether we move FCR by 0.1 is entirely unproven, and our feedback loop (water condition) is weaker than the incumbents' (watching the fish eat). Do not lead with this against a buyer who knows the sector.

tiers.claim: Earlier detection of deteriorating water quality
tiers.verifiability: MEDIUM — requires the farm to trust that what we detected mattered
tiers.proof_horizon: opportunistic; depends on something going wrong
tiers.status: the core technical claim, and the hardest to demonstrate on demand

tiers.verifiability: LOW — it is a counterfactual. Nobody can prove the kill that did not happen.
tiers.proof_horizon: years, and only statistically across many farms
tiers.why_it_is_last: It is the most emotionally compelling claim and the least demonstrable. It is also undermined by our own weather limitation: we cannot fly in the storms that trigger the worst events. Selling on catastrophe prevention writes a cheque the platform's flight envelope cannot cash.
tiers.status: should not be the headline claim until validated

the_value_hierarchy.the_strategic_conclusion: Sell tier 1 and 2. Earn tier 3. Prove tier 4. Never promise tier 5 before the data exists. This inverts the instinct of every founder in this category — everyone leads with the dead fish — and it is why most of them do not survive diligence.

differentiation.against_doing_nothing: A metered reduction in an existing electricity bill. Not a story about fish kills.
differentiation.against_a_fixed_buoy: Depth and coverage. And the honest admission that below a scale threshold, the buoy wins and we will say so.
differentiation.against_a_USV: Transit speed across many stations, simultaneous aerial imaging, and the ability to ignore dikes, nets, and cage structure entirely. Their endurance beats us and always will.
differentiation.against_satellite: Resolution, revisit rate, cloud immunity, and depth. And we should ingest their data rather than compete with it.
differentiation.against_salmon_platforms: Cost structure and tropical design case. Not technology — we would lose that fight.
differentiation.against_a_generic_drone_service: The probe. Anyone can fly a camera over a lake; calibrated depth-resolved chemistry from a hover is genuinely hard.

value_by_stakeholder.primary_value: Lower aeration cost, defensible numbers, audit records
Collective bargaining power in lake management, member protection, group insurability
Warning — but only if response capacity exists; the smallholder's primary AquaVision relationship is Phase 1, not this layer
Not making the pre-dawn boat run; evidence it was not his fault
value_by_stakeholder.pays: no, but can veto by neglect

value_by_stakeholder.primary_value: Continuous data for carrying capacity decisions they currently make blind

value_by_stakeholder.pays: not yet — needs loss history we do not have

the_honest_summary.what_we_can_defensibly_say_today: A phased platform where Phase 1 is a free app with no users yet, Phase 2 is a monetisation layer that cannot exist meaningfully before Phase 1 does, and Phase 3 is a hardware upgrade designed to profile water at depth across many stations at low marginal labour cost, with its own calibration ground truth on board, in an industry where nobody currently measures anything and fish die because of it.
the_honest_summary.what_we_cannot_say: That any of the three phases works yet. Phase 1 has not launched. Phase 2 has no data to run on. Phase 3 has not flown.
the_honest_summary.the_pitch_that_survives_diligence: Not 'we prevent fish kills' and not 'we have thousands of users.' Rather: three honestly-staged claims — free tools that should be checkably useful to a beginner from day one; a paid layer whose ROI a growing farm can verify against its own numbers once it exists; and a documented, recurring, expensive hardware-solvable failure mode at the top of the market, with the one thing we have to prove about each layer named plainly, and the boring, checkable value in each phase that pays its own way while the exciting claims are still being proven.
